rulings.Case Title: JOHN N. CHOKATOS VS. CHETCUTI & ASSOCIATES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 23, 2012, line 5. DEFENDANTS MAGNATE FUND #2, LLC, JOHN SIMONSE, MAGNATE FUND #1 LLC, MAGNATE FUND #3 LLC, JWS CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, INC., LHJS INVESTMENTS, 27TH STREET ASSOCIATES LLC, SOUTH VAN NESS ASSOCIATES LLC, 20 PARKRIDGE LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY AND 55 WOODWARD LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Second Amended Complaint. Of the allegations sought to be stricken, only the second sentence of paragraph 13 lacks a nexus to the alleged conspiracy to defraud. rulings.Case Title: JOHN N. CHOKATOS VS. CHETCUTI & ASSOCIATES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Magnate Fund #2, Llc, John Simonse, Magnate Fund #1 Llc, Magnate Fund #3 Llc, Jws Capital Management, Inc., Kghs Ubvestnebtsm 26tg Street Associates, Llc, 20 Parkridge Llc And 55 Woodward Llc'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 23, 2012, line 6. Defendants Magnate Fund #2, Llc, John Simonse, Magnate Fund #1 Llc, Magnate Fund #3 Llc, Jws Capital Management, Inc., Kghs Ubvestnebtsm 26tg Street Associates, Llc, 20 Parkridge Llc And 55 Woodward Llc'S Demurrer To Complaint. Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the second cause of action (Conversion) as to all defendants and as to the first, third, and fifth causes of action (Fraud, Financial Abuse of an Elder, and Declaratory Relief) as to defendants Magnate Fund #1 LLC, Magnate Fund #3 LLC, JWS Capital Management, LHJS Investments, 27th Street Associates LLC, South Van Ness Associates LLC, and 55 Woodward LLC, and overruled as to the fourth cause of action (Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress). As to the second cause of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege terms of the loan which show he is entitled to immediate possession of the disputed funds. As to the first, third and fifth causes of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege facts to show that defendants Magnate Fund #1 LLC, Magnate Fund #3 LLC, JWS Capital Management, LHJS Investments, 27th Street Associates LLC, South Van Ness Associates LLC, and 55 Woodward LLC were connected to the alleged fraud. The other claims are adequately pled. The order issued April 3, 2012 sustaining the demurrer was the result of a calendaring error and is vacated. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES S. MADOW, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF HIS VS. POST CONSTRUCTION SERVICES, L.P., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 23, 2012, line 11. CROSS DEFENDANT JAMES S. MADOW, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF HIS 401 (K) PLAN'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint. Granted. Mr. Madow is granted leave to file his amended answer on the condition that the trial date is continued at least three months to to a mutually agreeable date to enable the parties to take discovery on the new defenses. Expert discovery and percipient discovery as to the new defenses only will be triggered off the new trial date unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The parties to meet and confer to agree on a mutually convenient trial date and so inform the court at the hearing or, if the tentative ruling is not contested, by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. rulings.Case Title: LOAN DO VS. KHOA VAN DO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Admitted The Facts Set Forth In Pltf'S First Set Of Requests
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 23, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 1. PLAINTIFF LOAN DO'S Motion To Deem Admitted The Facts Set Forth In Plaintiff's First Set Of Requests. Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion is GRANTED. The facts set forth in Plaintiff's First Set of Requests for Admission to Defendant Wai Ming Lui are hereby deemed admitted. Within five (5) court days of service of this executed Order, Defendant Wai Ming Lui shall make payable to Plaintiff $279 for her reasonable attorney's fees and costs incurred in bringing this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to TSchoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
